title: 10 best herbal creams for reddened corners in mouth

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, soothing redness and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera contains antibacterial agents like cinnamic acid and phenolic compounds that help combat bacteria that can cause infection and inflammation, leading to reddened corners of the mouth.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's gel has been shown to promote collagen synthesis, improve tissue hydration, and accelerate wound healing, making it effective in reducing redness and irritation in the mouth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene and other antioxidants that help to reduce redness and irritation. When applied topically, aloe vera can help to calm and moisturize the skin, reducing the appearance of redness and promoting a healthy, balanced environment for the skin to heal and repair.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, making them beneficial for relieving redness and discomfort in the mouth.
Vitamins A, C, and E: These antioxidants help protect the mucous membranes from oxidative stress and promote healing, which can help alleviate redness and irritation in the mouth.
Glycoproteins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties and help protect the mucous membranes from damage, reducing redness and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are used to make creams for reddened corners in the mouth due to their rich content of aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from mature leaves by cutting off lower portion with a clean knife and scoop out gel.
Combine 1 cup of aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of distilled water in a blender.
Add 2 tablespoons of vegetable glycerin and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the blender.
Blend the mixture on high speed for 2 minutes then transfer it to a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly for 10-15 minutes until thickened.

Seeds: They are sometimes used due to their ability to provide a soothing and calming effect, which can help to reduce redness and irritation in mouth corners.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and set aside for infusion.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of boiling water and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl and discard solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of infused liquid in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and is smooth in texture within 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula flowers at peak bloom when they are fully open and have reached maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Mix 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the calendula's active properties.
Strain the infused oil and whip it with 1 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to preserve their oils.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at 120-140°f.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried flowers to 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan and heat at 160-170°f.
Combine the wax and oil mixture with 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Tea Tree Oil's antimicrobial activity can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may cause redness and inflammation in the mouth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a clean and sterile environment to ensure quality.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for 3-5 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Infuse the dried leaves in 500ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks or until the liquid turns green.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Mix 30g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter with 150ml of the infused oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale to make creams for reddened corners in the mouth because of their high concentration of gingerols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under running water for 5 minutes.
Grind 50g of the clean rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ground rhizome powder with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine 50g of coconut oil and 20g of distilled water with the beeswax mixture and heat for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour it into a glass jar for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 echinacea purpurea flowers and roots from a clean and dry area taking care not to damage them.
Dry the harvested flowers and roots in a warm place at 30-40 degrees celsius for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried flowers and roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax has fully dissolved and the mixture has cooled slightly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis from a reputable supplier.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the ground marshmallow root powder with 20 grams of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a bowl and allow it to cool at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and set it aside for later use.
Combine 20 milliliters of distilled water and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the beeswax mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Add the dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the melted beeswax mixture and stir for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before applying.

remedy_properties: Berberine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause inflammation and redness in the mouth.
Isatis: Isatis has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing redness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis roots and soak them in 500 milliliters of cold water for 8 hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Add 2 grams of beeswax and 2 grams of sweet almond oil to the liquid and stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat until it reaches a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit and maintain for 20 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes while occasionally stirring to facilitate gentle extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 200 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler and stir until fully melted.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Grind dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and store it in an airtight container.
Combine 20 grams of the thymus vulgaris powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Remove from heat, let cool to 120°f, and whip with an immersion blender for 10 seconds to create a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves measure out 20 grams for the infusion process.
Combine the harvested avena sativa leaves with 200 milliliters of boiling water allow it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids combine the liquid with 100 grams of unsalted butter melted to a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Stir in 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of vitamin e oil until fully incorporated maintain a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves, measuring exactly 50g, for subsequent infusion preparation.
Combine the harvested plantago major leaves with 200ml of cold-pressed organic olive oil, leaving them to steep for 48 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter, collecting the resulting extract and discarding solids within 30 minutes.
Mix the extracted plantago major oil with beeswax pellets, measuring 20g, and lanolin, measuring 10g, in a double boiler setup.
Stir the mixture for approximately 15 minutes as it cools to around 50°c, then transfer it into sterilized tubes or jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Wound-healing and tissue repair: Comfrey's allantoin content helps promote collagen synthesis, accelerating wound closure and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant: Comfrey's rosmarinic acid and triterpenoid saponins have potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm redness, swelling, and discomfort in the mouth.

Antimicrobial activity: Comfrey's flavonoids and terpenoids exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may contribute to the development of reddened corners in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the mouth.
Leaves: Comfrey leaves contain allantoin, a natural soothing agent that calms irritation and promotes healing in mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of symphytum officinale dried herb and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 50 milliliters of distilled water with 20 milliliters of glycerin in a heat-resistant glass mixing bowl.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer on a low flame for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 1 gram of vitamin e oil to the mixture, stirring constantly until the beeswax melts completely, within 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Pour the resulting cream into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before sealing and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, have been shown to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues. When applied topically to the affected area, dandelion cream can help calm the discomfort and redness associated with reddened corners in the mouth. Additionally, its antiseptic properties may also aid in preventing infection and promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the mouth by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage, helping to soothe and calm reddened areas in the mouth.

Astringent and anti-bacterial properties: Dandelion's astringent properties can help reduce excess moisture and bleeding in the mouth, while its anti-bacterial compounds can combat the growth of bacteria that may contribute to inflammation and infection in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, dandelion buds possess antibacterial and antifungal properties that combat oral infections causing redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at dawn or dusk for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of powdered taraxacum officinale with 20 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-proof glass measuring cup.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, then strain through a cheesecloth into a sterilized glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because they contain the anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of yarrow herb. The cream soothes and calms irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling associated with canker sores or minor oral irritations. Yarrow's antimicrobial properties also help to prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate mouth sores, promoting a healthy recovery. Additionally, yarrow creams may help to reduce pain and discomfort by numbing the affected area, providing relief from redness and inflammation in the corners of the mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow creams contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the mouth, calming irritated tissues and soothing discomfort.

Antiseptic: The antimicrobial compounds present in yarrow, such as thymol and camphor, exhibit antiseptic properties, preventing infection and promoting healing by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may contribute to mouth sores and redness.
Astringent: Yarrow's astringent properties help tighten and shrink blood vessels, reducing the appearance of redness and swelling in the mouth, while also helping to dry up excess saliva and mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation in the oral cavity.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems, cutting them into small pieces for drying.
Dry the cut achillea millefolium pieces using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 3-4 hours or air-drying method.
Combine dried achillea millefolium with 250ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar, stirring well to ensure uniform mixture.
Steep the herbal mixture in a double boiler setup for 2 hours at low heat, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid cream base to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sagebrush creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb sage, native to the Great Basin region where sagebrush grows, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. When applied topically as a cream or ointment, sagebrush's compounds work to reduce redness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. As a result, reddened corners in the mouth can be relieved, providing instant comfort and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage leaves contain compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage extracts contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help alleviate redness and swelling by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.
Wound-healing properties: Sage leaves contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which may promote wound healing and tissue repair, helping to reduce the appearance of redness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated oral tissues.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, the barks provide astringent and antibacterial properties to reduce redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia tridentata leaves in late summer or early fall to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves using a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours or a dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried artemisia tridentata with 100ml of distilled water in a glass jar to make a tea-like infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes and then strain it using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and sediment.
Mix the liquid extract with 200g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler, stirring until fully incorporated and melted.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for reddened corners in mouth are topical treatments that help alleviate redness, irritation, and discomfort caused by inflamed mouth corners. These creams are derived from natural herbs that possess anti-inflammatory, soothing, and protective properties. The best herbal creams for this issue include Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Arnica montana, Aloe ferox, and Zingiber officinale. If left untreated, reddened mouth corners can lead to pain while eating, speaking, and even sleeping, negatively impacting daily life, causing social withdrawal, and affecting overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to reddened corners in mouth that are treated with herbal creams are angular cheilitis, stomatitis, and aphthous ulcers. These conditions cause inflammation, redness, and painful sores or cracks on the lips and mouth. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can help soothe and calm these irritations, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with reddened corners in mouth is Aloe vera and tea tree oil-based cream. Aloe vera soothes and calms the irritated area, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Tea tree oil has antiseptic properties that combat bacterial and fungal infections, preventing further irritation and promoting a faster recovery. Mixing aloe vera with tea tree oil-based cream can provide a powerful and natural remedy to heal reddened corners in the mouth, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, calendula's antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause mouth ulcers and redness, promoting a faster healing process and alleviating discomfort. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for soothing mouth irritations.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help reduce inflammation and promote healing by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which help reduce redness and swelling by neutralizing free radicals and inhibiting the activation of inflammatory cells.
Carotenoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help protect the affected area from infections and reduce redness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the mouth.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide a protective barrier and reduce inflammation in the affected areas.
Roots: They are used due to their rich content of saponins and other bioactive compounds that contribute to the overall soothing and healing properties of the cream.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the plant material.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted then remove from the heat.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean container and let it set for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because they contain comfrey's anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which effectively reduce swelling and irritation. The herb's ability to promote tissue repair and regeneration also aids in healing the affected area, minimizing the appearance of redness and discomfort. As a result, the cream provides quick relief and promotes a faster recovery, helping to restore the mouth's natural appearance and functionality. It's also a natural remedy for mouth sores and ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in soothing and calming reddened mouth corners.
Alkaloids (including symphytine): Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, alleviating reddened mouth corners.
Tannins: Possess astringent properties, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, thereby easing the appearance of reddened mouth corners.
remedy_parts: Leaves:
Roots:
Flowers:
remedy_recipe: Extract the root of symphytum officinale by digging up the plant with a spade and separating the roots from the rest of the plant.
Dry the extracted root in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and then pour it into a container to cool and set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's ability to boost the immune system and fight off infections also reduces the severity of mouth ulcers and canker sores. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's antioxidant properties help to protect against cell damage and promote healthy cell growth, which can aid in the healing process and reduce the appearance of redness and inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the mouth, alleviating discomfort.
Alkylamides: These constituents exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the body's immune response and fight off the underlying cause of the reddened mouth corners.
Flavonoids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth, leading to a reduction in redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of immunomodulatory compounds that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other antioxidants that may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in the affected areas.
Flowers: They are rich in alkaloids and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate redness and discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create a smooth consistency.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to create a smooth and soothing echinacea purpurea cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing. When applied to the affected area, aloe vera cream can help to calm the skin, reduce redness and irritation, and promote the growth of new cells, making it an effective remedy for soothing red corners in the mouth.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the mouth, thereby alleviating redness and discomfort.
Flavonoids: With their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, flavonoids help protect the mouth tissues from further damage and promote healing of reddened areas.
Saponins: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections and promote the healing of mouth sores, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is used due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and reduce irritation in the mouth.
Stems: The juice from Aloe vera stems contains compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease pain, making it effective in treating redness and inflammation in the mouth.

Gel from Leaves: The thick, clear gel found inside the leaves of Aloe vera is widely used in creams due to its ability to hydrate and protect the skin, reducing redness and discomfort in the mouth corners.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe vera gel and add 20 grams of coconut oil to a mixing bowl.
Mix the aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the bowl from heat and stir in 10 grams of beeswax until fully dissolved.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of shea butter to the bowl and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene and other antioxidants, which work to reduce inflammation and promote healing. These properties help to calm irritation and redness, reducing the discomfort associated with reddened mouth corners. Additionally, aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to keep the skin hydrated and healthy, further contributing to its ability to alleviate this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, soothing the reddened corners of the mouth.
Glycoproteins: These aid in the regeneration of skin cells, facilitating faster recovery and reducing redness in the mouth corners.
Fatty acids: These have anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with reddened mouth corners, promoting a soothing environment for healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracted from Aloe vera leaves are used to create soothing creams due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and hydrating properties, which help calm irritated tissues.
Gel from Leaves: Specifically, the clear gel inside the leaves is extracted to create creams for its ability to heal and moisturize the skin, reducing redness and inflammation in the mouth.
Pulp from Leaves: The pulp of Aloe vera leaves is also used to create creams due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that promote skin health and reduce redness in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 large aloe barbadensis leaves and clean them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Extract the gel from the aloe leaves using a spoon or a sterile utensil and collect 1 cup of the pure gel.
Mix the aloe gel with 2 tablespoons of distilled water and 2 teaspoons of vegetable glycerin in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 120-130°f.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and allow it to cool completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil, a key component in these creams, has the ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. This leads to faster healing and regeneration of the skin cells. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of Melaleuca alternifolia help to prevent infection, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover and reducing the appearance of reddened corners in the mouth.
remedy_constituents: Cineol: This compound exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe the affected areas in the mouth.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has antimicrobial properties, which help combat the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause redness and irritation in the mouth.
Linalool: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve pain and discomfort associated with reddened corners in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm reddened areas.
Buds: The buds are used for their high oil content, which is similar to tea tree oil and has similar benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting infection.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in a clean glass container.
Combine the weighed leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 20 minutes at a temperature of 60 to 70 degrees celsius then remove from heat.
Allow the infused mixture to steep for 2 hours then strain it using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix the strained infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil then whip until creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The licorice root extract in the cream has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra help to reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective remedy for mouth sores and reddened corners. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can provide long-lasting relief and promote healthy oral tissue.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, thereby alleviating the redness and discomfort associated with mouth corners.
Flavonoids: Possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect the sensitive skin around the mouth, reducing redness and inflammation.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help to combat infections and soothe irritated tissues, thereby reducing redness and discomfort in the mouth corners.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in the roots, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated oral tissues.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing mouth redness.
Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra is also used due to its rich content of glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in 1 liter of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then combine it with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes then add 100g of coconut oil and 100g of shea butter.
Continue heating the mixture for another 30 minutes then remove it from the heat and let it cool.
Once cooled whip the mixture with 20g of vitamin e oil and 10g of lavender oil until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. The moisturizing and emollient properties of the cream also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. As a result, the cream can provide relief from painful mouth corners and promote a healthy, irritation-free environment.
remedy_constituents: Alkamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness associated with mouth sores and inflammation.
Acidic phenols: These phenolic compounds exhibit strong antioxidant properties that help protect the oral mucosa from oxidative stress, promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation.

Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce redness and swelling in the mouth, while also promoting tissue repair and protecting against oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most used part due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which provide anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: They are also used in creams due to their similar composition to flowers, offering anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, making them a popular choice for creams, although less common than flowers or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried arnica montana flowers and leaves using 500 milliliters of carrier oil like sunflower oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 100 degrees fahrenheit to facilitate the infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then let it cool to room temperature.
Mix the infused oil with 30 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens into a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe ferox creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because its soothing properties and high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids help to calm and heal irritated tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties present in Aloe ferox cream reduce swelling and redness, while its moisturizing properties help to hydrate and soften the skin. This results in reduced discomfort and faster healing, providing relief from the pain and tenderness associated with reddened corners in the mouth. It also promotes a healthy oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes, which can contribute to the relief of reddened corners in the mouth.
Amino acids (such as Glycine and Alanine): These amino acids can aid in the healing and regeneration of damaged oral tissues, helping to alleviate redness and discomfort in the mouth.
Flavonoids (such as Quercetin): These plant-derived compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the appearance of redness and swelling in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe ferox leaves is commonly used in creams due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and irritation in the mouth.
Stems: Aloe ferox stem gel is also used to make creams, as it contains similar compounds to the leaf gel, providing anti-inflammatory and soothing effects for oral health.

Roots: Some studies suggest that the root of Aloe ferox may contain bioactive compounds that can help in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a potential ingredient in creams for oral care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 kg of aloe ferox leaves from mature plants on a sunny day.
Clean and peel the leaves to remove outer skin and bitter compounds.
Grate 1.5 kg of the peeled aloe ferox flesh into a large stainless steel bowl.
Mix 1 kg of coconut oil with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler at 60°c.
Combine the grated aloe ferox mixture with the melted coconut oil mixture at 70°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with reddened corners in mouth because they contain anti-inflammatory properties of ginger, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The antimicrobial properties of ginger in these creams combat infections and reduce inflammation, promoting healthy tissue repair. As a result, the redness and irritation associated with canker sores or mouth ulcers begin to subside, allowing the affected area to heal faster and return to its normal state. This natural remedy provides effective relief from mouth sores.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the mouth by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with reddened mouth corners.
Zingerone: Zingerone has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the mouth tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing redness and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams for reddened corners in mouth because they possess antiseptic and soothing properties.
Buds: Zingiber officinale buds are used to make creams for reddened corners in mouth due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried zingiber officinale root and grind it into fine powder.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of sweet almond oil to the mixture.
Stir in 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of aloe vera gel.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for about 30 minutes before transferring to jars.
